title: Content frontmatter schema + structured program/eligibility-factor data model + CI schema validation
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This task defines the typed content frontmatter and structured program/eligibility-factor data model plus CI validation, enforcing the route-to-human and citation gates in schema so no guide can ship without an official-application link, a free-help pointer, and a citation on every claim.
objective: Implement the typed content frontmatter schema and structured program/eligibility-factor data model with CI schema validation that fails on schema-invalid content or any uncited claim.
acceptanceCriteria: Typed frontmatter schema per guide: program, jurisdiction, audience, plain-language summary, eligibility factors (described, not a determination), how-to-apply steps, documents, pitfalls, officialApplicationUrl, freeHelpPointers[], and a citations[] array with provenance fields
Structured program/eligibility-factor data model with lastVerified/validUntil on every sourced figure; CI fails on schema-invalid content or any claim missing a citation
Schema enforces presence of an official-application link AND >= 1 free-help pointer on every guide (the route-to-human coverage gate)
